# UC-18.4.9 ¬∑ Nexus Dashboard Insights Site and Fabric Composite Assurance Health Score Trending

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Performance, Capacity, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a rolling wellness number for each fabric in your halls, like a report card for the switching grid, so teams shore up weak spots before anyone feels slow service.*

---

## Description

Monitors Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights composite Assurance health scores (the 0‚Äì100 roll-up per site and fabric) plus pillar-style driver categories derived from correlated findings, highlighting sustained score decay, volatile high‚Äìlow spreads, and critical/major finding surges that explain why the executive headline number moved.

## Value

Leadership and operations share one trusted trend line for fabric risk before customer-impacting faults accumulate, letting teams prioritize spine upgrades, capacity buys, or compliance clean-up with audited Splunk history that outlasts the on-appliance UI and aligns CAB decisions with measurable score recovery.

## Implementation

(1) Land `cisco:nd:site_health` via Splunkbase 1546 against `/telemetry/v2/nodes/{site}/health` plus companion `cisco:nd:findings` feeds. (2) Schedule the SPL with maintenance lookups. (3) Wire alerts to Insights deep links and UC-18.4.1 drilldowns.

## Evidence

Saved search `svc_ndi_fabric_health_score_watch`, Dashboard panel `DC_Fabric_Assurance_HealthScore_18_4_9`, and weekly CSV exports archived to `network_evidence` index with 13-month retention.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Lab fabric lowers site_health from 95 to 72 over six hours while cisco:nd:findings simultaneously emits ten critical-ish capacity rows tagged category=capacity; SPL returns P1_score_collapse with has_capacity=1 and non-null driver_categories.

### Negative scenario

Approved maintenance window lists the same site_key in maintenance_allow_list with valid_until covering the search window; score dips to 80 but alert logic suppresses notification per lookup filter, leaving only dashboard drift for auditors.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Nexus Dashboard appliances (ND-STARTER, ND-3K, virtual OVA) host the Nexus Dashboard Insights 6.x application with Assurance entitlements so Insights can emit composite health scores (0‚Äì100) per registered site and fabric, derived from weighted findings in the pillars Connectivity, Best Practices, Capacity, Change Impact, and Compliance described in Cisco‚Äôs Nexus Dashboard Insights documentation set.
- Splunk 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud must reach GET `https://<nexus-dashboard>/api/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/v2/nodes/{site}/health` (resolve `{site}` via GET `/api/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/v2/config/insightsGroup`) over 443/tcp; attach certificates to heavy forwarders when Insights cannot open outbound HTTPS directly.
- Install cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk (**Splunkbase 7777**) for dashboards and macros, and Splunk REST API Modular Input (**Splunkbase 1546**) to poll health JSON into **`index=cisco_dc`** with **`sourcetype=cisco:nd:site_health`**; ingest **`cisco:nd:findings`**, **`cisco:nd:advisory`**, **`cisco:nd:compliance`**, and **`cisco:nd:syslog`** beside it for corroboration.
- Service accounts need telemetry v2 read access on nodes and insightsGroup paths; map LDAP/SAML roles (Site Manager, Approver) to least-privilege tokens stored in Splunk‚Äôs credential store.
- Budget roughly one **`site_health` event per site per poll** (commonly 15‚Äì60 minutes) plus bursts of `findings` after changes; size retention for 90‚Äì180 days of trend evidence.

### Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Create a REST modular input (1546) stanza per Insights cluster with response_type JSON, url pointing at `/api/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/v2/nodes/*/health` loops orchestrated by a small wrapper (Insights expects one site per call), headers carrying Bearer tokens, and polling_interval ‚â• 300 seconds to avoid HTTP 429 backoff storms.
(2) Map payloads to **`site_name`**, **`fabric_name`**, **`health_score`** (duplicate **`assurance_score`**, **`composite_score`**, `overallScore` aliases in props.conf), **`risk_level`**, **`open_findings`**, and optional pillar penalty arrays; keep ISO-8601 timestamps intact for correlation.
(3) Enable findings streaming into **`sourcetype=cisco:nd:findings`** so `category` fields align with Assurance pillar vocabulary; delay alerts until both feeds land in the same index.
(4) Forward `cisco:nd:advisory`, `cisco:nd:compliance`, `cisco:nd:syslog` with shared `site` metadata to differentiate platform outages from fabric scoring drift.
(5) Document poll cadence, proxy exceptions, and token rotation inside the NetOps runbook beside Maintenance windows.

### Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the SPL from the `spl` field on a 48-hour window for operations and 7-day variants for leadership dashboards. Understanding this SPL: the query intentionally mixes **`site_health`** rows with `findings` rows so composite score erosion surfaces alongside **driver_categories**‚Äîunlike **UC-18.4.1**, which emphasizes discrete anomaly events, this pattern tracks the rolled-up Assurance score operators see in Insights executive tiles. **`site_key`** / **`fabric_key`** coalesce hedges camelCase vs snake_case exports. The `stats` clause computes **`last_health`**, **`min_health`**, **`max_health`**, and **`health_spread`** to catch volatile fabrics even when the latest snapshot looks acceptable. **`sev_findings`** counts Critical/Major class rows only; tune `match` if your deployment labels severities differently. `priority` encodes paging policy: P1 for collapse, P2 for storms, P3 for actionable drift. Schedule alerting every 30 minutes with 30-minute per-site throttle; attach a lookup **`maintenance_allow_list.csv`** (`where NOT [| inputlookup maintenance_allow_list | fields site_key]`) so CAB-approved work suppresses benign dips.

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nd:findings") earliest=-48h
| eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(site_name,siteName,site_id,node_name,nd_site)))
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric_id,nd_fabric)))
| eval health=tonumber(coalesce(health_score,assurance_score,fabric_health,composite_score,overallScore,fabricScore,nirHealthScore))
| eval fcat=lower(trim(coalesce(finding_category,category,insight_category,penalty_bucket,pillar)))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,Severity,finding_severity)))
| eval is_health=if(match(sourcetype,"^cisco:nd:site_health$"),1,0)
| eval is_find=if(match(sourcetype,"^cisco:nd:findings$"),1,0)
| eval criticalish=if(is_find==1 AND match(sev,"(?i)critical|major|high|blocker"),1,0)
| stats
    latest(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS last_health,
    min(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS min_health,
    max(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS max_health,
    earliest(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS first_health,
    sum(criticalish) AS sev_findings,
    values(eval(if(is_find==1 AND len(fcat)>0,fcat,null))) AS driver_categories
  BY site_key fabric_key
| fillnull value=0 sev_findings
| eval health_spread=round(max_health-min_health,2)
| eval delta_window=round(last_health-first_health,2)
| eval sla_gap=round(90-last_health,2)
| eval priority=case(
    last_health<70 OR health_spread>25,"P1_score_collapse",
    last_health<80 AND sev_findings>5,"P2_category_storm",
    last_health<87 OR sev_findings>0 OR health_spread>12 OR delta_window<-8,"P3_actionable",
    1=1,"P4_watch")
| where priority IN ("P1_score_collapse","P2_category_storm","P3_actionable")
| eval has_connectivity=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"connectivity")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"link")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_capacity=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"capacity")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"resource")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_change=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"change")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"impact")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_compliance=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"compliance")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"best")>=0,1,0)
| eval narrative=case(
    priority=="P1_score_collapse","Escalate to fabric owner; open Assurance executive view for the site.",
    priority=="P2_category_storm","Triage findings categories before hardware swaps; coordinate with change record owner.",
    1=1,"Review driver_categories against approved maintenance and capacity forecasts.")
| table site_key,fabric_key,last_health,min_health,max_health,health_spread,delta_window,sla_gap,sev_findings,driver_categories,has_connectivity,has_capacity,has_change,has_compliance,priority,narrative
| sort last_health delta_window -sev_findings
| head 150
```

Pipeline walkthrough
- Stage 1 scopes `index=cisco_dc` and the paired sourcetypes within **48h**‚Äîextend to **168h** when proving weekly regression.
- Stage 2 normalizes identities before aggregation to stop duplicate rows when ND exports both UUID and friendly labels.
- Stage 3 aggregates health vs findings into one row per **site_key**/**fabric_key**.
- Stage 4 applies `priority` filters that define alert candidates.
- Stage 5 enriches booleans for quick filters in **Dashboard Studio** Radios (Connectivity vs Capacity vs Change Impact vs Compliance drivers).

### Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
(1) Open **Nexus Dashboard Insights ‚Üí Assurance ‚Üí Site Health**, pick a reference site, and compare the score to `last_health` at the same hour; discrepancies usually mean timezone offsets or duplicated **friendly names**‚Äîfix with **`nd_site_id`** lookups.
(2) Run **`timechart span=1h count by sourcetype` for **`cisco:nd:site_health`**; flat lines longer than **2√ó** the poll interval imply **REST** failure‚Äîtrace **splunkd** logs on the forwarder executing **1546**.
(3) Spot-check two **Criticalfindings** in the UI against raw `cisco:nd:findings` events; **`object_name`** and **`category`** must match or field extractions need retuning.
(4) When **`Compliance`** appears in **`driver_categories`**, compare counts to `cisco:nd:compliance` panels to ensure template evaluations (covered in **UC-18.4.10**) are not confused with composite score penalties here.
(5) Confirm `cisco:nd:advisory` spikes align with documentation refreshes rather than sudden fabric faults‚Äîexecutive scores should stabilise after **Insights** finishes metadata ingestion.

### Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
(1) **Executive** dashboard row: **area chart** of average `last_health` per `fabric_key` with reference band **90‚Äì100** and annotations for CAB milestones.
(2) **Operator** table: sort by **`priority`** then `sev_findings`, with drilldown saved search launching **UC-18.4.1** SPL filtered on the same `site_key`.
(3) Add capacity overlays by joining (via **`lookup`**) **`has_capacity=1`** rows to interface utilization macros bundled in **7777**.
(4) Restrict **index** access to network operations and compliance roles; redact bearer tokens from **searchURL** parameters by keeping credentials solely inside modular inputs.
(5) Archive weekly **CSV** exports for **Operational** reviews linking **score** slope to change tickets.

### Step 5 ‚Äî Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting
Visualization ‚Äî Pair a **timechartof`last_health`** (span=4h) with a **column chartof`sev_findings`**; add a **single-value** panel for worst **`sla_gap`**; use a **treemap** after **`mvexpand driver_categories`** for pillar share during postmortems.

Alert design ‚Äî **Priority P1**: page **fabric** anchor + **duty manager**; include **deeplink** tokens to **InsightsUUID.Priority P2**: notify **automation** channel with `driver_categories` list; require acknowledgement before **suppression.Priority P3**: email digest only if outside **`maintenance_allow_list`**. Tie **Compliance**-heavy drops to governance queues, **Connectivity** drops to optical **on-call**.

Troubleshooting
- **NULL `last_health` everywhere‚ÜíJSON** path drift after **Insights** upgrade; refresh **FIELDALIAS** and retest with **`spath`.
- HTTP 401/403** on modular input ‚Üí rotate **API** credentials; confirm **SAML** groups still map to **telemetryreaders.
- HTTP 429** ‚Üí increase **polling_interval** or shard **sites** across modular inputs.
- **Scores stuck at 100** while **`findings`** explode ‚Üí **telemetry** worker backlog on Nexus Dashboard; verify **kubectl top pods** or Cisco TAC **ND** health scripts before blaming **Splunk.
- Fewer `site_health` events than inventory sites‚ÜíinsightsGroup** missing newly onboarded fabrics‚Äîre-import from **APIC** or **NDFC.
- `site_key` collisions** ‚Üí enforce canonical `nd_site_id` from CMDB via **`lookup`.
- All categories empty but score drops** ‚Üí penalties embedded only in `site_health` payload‚Äîinspect raw JSON for **nested penalty** arrays.


## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nd:findings") earliest=-48h
| eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(site_name,siteName,site_id,node_name,nd_site)))
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric_id,nd_fabric)))
| eval health=tonumber(coalesce(health_score,assurance_score,fabric_health,composite_score,overallScore,fabricScore,nirHealthScore))
| eval fcat=lower(trim(coalesce(finding_category,category,insight_category,penalty_bucket,pillar)))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,Severity,finding_severity)))
| eval is_health=if(match(sourcetype,"^cisco:nd:site_health$"),1,0)
| eval is_find=if(match(sourcetype,"^cisco:nd:findings$"),1,0)
| eval criticalish=if(is_find==1 AND match(sev,"(?i)critical|major|high|blocker"),1,0)
| stats
    latest(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS last_health,
    min(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS min_health,
    max(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS max_health,
    earliest(eval(if(is_health==1,health,null))) AS first_health,
    sum(criticalish) AS sev_findings,
    values(eval(if(is_find==1 AND len(fcat)>0,fcat,null))) AS driver_categories
  BY site_key fabric_key
| fillnull value=0 sev_findings
| eval health_spread=round(max_health-min_health,2)
| eval delta_window=round(last_health-first_health,2)
| eval sla_gap=round(90-last_health,2)
| eval priority=case(
    last_health<70 OR health_spread>25,"P1_score_collapse",
    last_health<80 AND sev_findings>5,"P2_category_storm",
    last_health<87 OR sev_findings>0 OR health_spread>12 OR delta_window<-8,"P3_actionable",
    1=1,"P4_watch")
| where priority IN ("P1_score_collapse","P2_category_storm","P3_actionable")
| eval has_connectivity=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"connectivity")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"link")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_capacity=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"capacity")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"resource")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_change=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"change")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"impact")>=0,1,0)
| eval has_compliance=if(mvfind(driver_categories,"compliance")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_categories,"best")>=0,1,0)
| eval narrative=case(
    priority=="P1_score_collapse","Escalate to fabric owner; open Assurance executive view for the site.",
    priority=="P2_category_storm","Triage findings categories before hardware swaps; coordinate with change record owner.",
    1=1,"Review driver_categories against approved maintenance and capacity forecasts.")
| table site_key,fabric_key,last_health,min_health,max_health,health_spread,delta_window,sla_gap,sev_findings,driver_categories,has_connectivity,has_capacity,has_change,has_compliance,priority,narrative
| sort last_health delta_window -sev_findings
| head 150
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart of avg last_health by fabric with 90 reference line; (2) Table of priority, sev_findings, driver_categories; (3) Treemap of expanded categories after incidents; (4) Single-value worst sla_gap tile.

## Known False Positives

**healthscore_bootstrap_window** - Fresh **site** onboarding keeps **composite** **scores** artificially high until **Insights** finishes first-pass **connectivity** sweeps; **where** clauses should ignore **P3** drops for **72h** after **`site_provision_date`** from **`lookup nd_site_rollout.csv`** or compare against **`insightsGroup firstSeen`** timestamps before paging.

**assurance_weighting_metadata_refresh** - Cisco bug-metadata or **PSIRT** library updates can re-weight **minor** findings overnight, producing **single-digit** score steps without new faults; tie **`delta_window`** alerts to a non-zero **`cisco:nd:findings`** event rate and cross-check **Insights** release notes; **suppress** via **`lookup penalty_release_ack.csv`** stamped by engineering.

**capacity_planning_whatif** - Operators run **capacity simulation** tasks that temporarily inflate **Capacity** pillar penalties; validate active **what-if** jobs in the **Insights** UI and filter **`has_capacity=1`** alerts with **`changereq_open=0`** from **`inputlookup cab_staging.csv`**.

**nd_appservices_queue_storm** - **Nexus Dashboard** microservices restarting after patching can stall **health** polling while **findings** ingestion continues; correlate **`cisco:nd:syslog`** **ERROR** bursts with score freezes; **suppress** **P1** if **`kubectl get pods`** equivalents show rolling restarts tied to approved **CHG** tokens.

**fabric_scope_partial_resync** - Sometimes only half the **spine** cluster registers during **NDFC** sync, skewing **Best Practices**; confirm **inventory counts** in **Insights** match **CMDB** before treating **`last_health`** as production truth; pause alerts using **`lookup fabric_resync_in_progress.csv`**.

**change_impact_shadow_penalties** - Planned **ISSU** or **maintenance** modes inject **Change Impact** deductions that self-heal; require **`maintenance_allow_list`** matches with **time-bound** **`valid_until`** columns before notifying leadership.

**duplicate_poll_json_merge** - Misconfigured **1546** inputs may **duplicate** **site_health** events with identical timestamps, exaggerating volatility; **dedup** **`site_key fabric_key _time`** in a **summary index** or fix modular input **tracking** keys when **`health_spread`** looks implausible above **40**.

**cross_fabric_label_collision** - Identical **`fabric_name`** strings in disjoint regions can merge wrongly; enforce **`region_code`** prefixes inside **`fabric_key`** using **`lookup site_region.csv`** before **`stats`** aggregation triggers false **P2** storms.
